claims.claim: We evaluate whether non-uniform (global/ragged) REAP pruning with manual FP8/BF16 scoring yields lower prompt-level loss than uniform baseline pruning on Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B.

claims.claim: In the 16-prompt suite, global/ragged pruning produced a mean loss of 10.892 versus uniform 10.902 (Δ = −0.0098), but the paired bootstrap confidence interval crossed zero.

claims.claim: Mixture-of-experts (MoE) models such as Qwen3-Coder-30B-A3B activate only a subset of expert parameters per token, creating an opportunity for non-uniform pruning strategies that exploit the heterogeneous importance of different experts.
